topic: Summerlin South Handyman: High-Ticket Repair Packages for Luxury Homes, Tight Finishes, and Desert Wear
excerpt: Summerlin sits on the western rim of the Las Vegas Valley, and Summerlin South carries a distinctly upscale housing mix, including luxury enclaves like The Ridges, so repairs have to look clean under bright light and hold up under heavy sun exposure. Underneath, the valley’s native soils are shallow, well-drained alluvial soils with carbonates/caliche, which is why small movement can show up as drifting doors, recurring drywall cracks, or gates that go a little out of square even when the house feels structurally fine. Then the climate does the rest: triple-digit summer heat, strong UV, and monsoon-season storm swings dry out sealants, stress trim joints, and expose any weak drainage detail in a hurry. Around here, the durable fix is usually a high-ticket bundled scope: tune the openings, reinforce the finish work, correct the water path, and blend everything so it doesn’t look like three different repair crews took a crack at it.
markdown_tables:
| High-ticket Summerlin South package | What it usually includes | Representative total |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Luxury punch-list reset | doors, hardware, wall repair, trim details, caulk, second-pass cleanup | $3,000–$8,500 |
| Multi-room wall and ceiling finish package | crack repair, wide floats, texture blending, premium finish prep | $2,500–$9,500 |
| Exterior trim and seal package | sun-baked trim repairs, resealing, fascia/soffit touchpoints, paint-ready prep | $3,000–$10,500 |
| Gate and perimeter package | gate alignment, post/rail corrections, hardware upgrades, clean close/latch | $2,500–$9,000 |
| Pre-listing “showing ready” scope | high-visibility fixes inside and out, plus final walk-through punch | $4,000–$14,000 |
| Smart-home + entry package | lock/doorbell/camera installs, strike tuning, sealing, trim detail cleanup | $2,500–$8,000 |
